response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position demands deep, sustained C++ expertise for industrial automation systems, whereas his C++ exposure is limited to a single 6-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project. His primary stack is TypeScript, JavaScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of experience with industrial protocols, IEC 62443 security standards, MQTT, or embedded/IoT systems, which are core to this position.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with event-driven and high-throughput architectures (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/sec using async I/O and fibers)
Solid software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and agile methodologies across multiple roles
Full-stack breadth (Node.js, React, cloud deployment) covers the listed advantageous skills
concerns: C++ proficiency is superficial and dated — only 6 months at National Instruments (2015) and a thesis project; no sustained systems-level C++ career track
Zero exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443 security standards, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
No evidence of multi-threaded systems design, message-broker architectures, or embedded/IoT development in C++
